data.title: Israel's Central Bank Governor Yaron: Inflation Expected to Ease by End of 2026
data.description: Israel's Central Bank Governor Amir Yaron has projected that inflation will slow down by the end of 2026. According to Jin10, Yaron's forecast comes amid ongoing efforts to manage economic stability and control inflationary pressures. The central bank is closely monitoring economic indicators to ensure that inflation targets are met within the projected timeline. This outlook reflects the bank's commitment to maintaining financial stability and supporting sustainable economic growth in the coming years.

data.title: Fed Governor Milan: Inflation Expectations Unaffected by Rising Oil Prices
data.description: According to Jin10, Federal Reserve Governor Milan stated that the recent increase in oil prices has not yet impacted inflation expectations. Milan emphasized that while oil prices have risen, the broader inflation outlook remains stable. The Fed continues to monitor economic indicators closely to assess any potential changes in inflation trends. Milan's comments come amid ongoing discussions about the effects of energy prices on the economy and monetary policy.

data.title: Mexico's Central Bank Governor: Rate Cut Cycle Nears End
data.description: According to Jin10, the Governor of Mexico's Central Bank has announced that the country's cycle of interest rate cuts is approaching its conclusion. This statement comes amid ongoing evaluations of the economic landscape and monetary policy adjustments. The central bank has been actively managing interest rates to address economic challenges and stimulate growth. As the cycle nears its end, future monetary policy decisions will likely focus on maintaining economic stability and addressing inflationary pressures.

data.title: Federal Reserve Governor Milan: Inflation expectations have not yet been affected by rising oil prices.
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Milan stated that inflation expectations have not yet been affected by rising oil prices. (Jinshi)

data.title: Mitsubishi UFJ Analysts: Iran Conflict Could Weaken GBP Against CHF
data.description: According to Jin10, Mitsubishi UFJ analysts have indicated in a report that the British pound may weaken against the Swiss franc if the conflict in Iran leads to a further decline in risk appetite. They noted that as market focus shifts from the inflationary impact of rising energy prices to concerns over economic growth, risk aversion may intensify. This would put pressure on the pound, compounded by market worries about stagflation in the UK. In this scenario, these factors would outweigh the previous support for the pound from expectations of Bank of England rate hikes. Meanwhile, the relatively underperforming Swiss franc is expected to benefit from its safe-haven status.

data.title: Singapore Ranked as Most Energy-Vulnerable Country in Recent Study
data.description: Singapore has been identified as the most energy-vulnerable nation among 75 countries, according to a study by Energy World Mag in March 2026. According to NS3.AI, the study assigned Singapore a vulnerability score of 85.2. The report highlights that Singapore relies on fossil fuels for nearly 98% of its energy needs and imports all of its natural gas.

data.title: Launch of Metals.io on Tezos Expands Tokenized Commodities Market
data.description: Trilitech has introduced Metals.io on the Tezos blockchain, furthering its initiative in the tokenized commodities sector. According to NS3.AI, the platform, which debuted on Monday, initially offers tokenized uranium, tokenized gold, and Noemon Tech's RARE token. This launch follows the success of Uranium.io, which has attracted approximately 9,000 retail investors. Earlier this year, a partnership with Transak enabled retail investors to purchase tokenized uranium using cryptocurrency or credit cards, with a minimum investment of $10, significantly lower than the previous $4.2 million over-the-counter market threshold.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Alibaba Unveils Qwen3.5-Omni Multimodal Model
data.description: Alibaba has announced the launch of its Qwen3.5-Omni multimodal model. According to PANews, the Qwen3.5-Omni series includes Plus, Flash, and Light versions, all of which support a 256k long context. The model can handle over 10 hours of audio input and more than 400 seconds of 720P (1FPS) audio-visual input. It has undergone extensive multimodal pre-training on vast amounts of text, visual data, and over 100 million hours of audio-visual data, showcasing exceptional multimodal perception and generation capabilities. Compared to its predecessor, Qwen3-Omni, the Qwen3.5-Omni model has significantly enhanced multilingual capabilities, supporting speech recognition in 113 languages and dialects and speech generation in 36 languages and dialects.

data.title: US Secretary of State: Iran's permanent control of the Strait of Hormuz will never be allowed.
data.description: On March 30 local time, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated that the US would not allow Iran to permanently control the Strait of Hormuz or establish a toll system. In a media interview that day, Rubio said the US aims to achieve its military objectives against Iran "within weeks, not months." He said, "He (US President Trump) favors a diplomatic approach. These efforts are still in their early stages. Some negotiations are underway, including through intermediaries." Rubio added, "But we must also be prepared for these efforts to potentially fail," and that Iran "is threatening to permanently control the Strait of Hormuz and establish a toll system, etc. This must not be allowed to happen." (CCTV News)

data.title: Trump Administration Proposes Enhanced Legal Protections for Alternative Assets in Retirement Plans
data.description: The Trump administration has introduced a proposal aimed at providing increased legal protection for companies that offer alternative assets in retirement savings plans. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential impact of this proposal on the retirement savings industry. The initiative seeks to encourage diversification in retirement portfolios by safeguarding firms that include non-traditional assets such as real estate, private equity, and hedge funds. This move is part of a broader effort to expand investment options for retirement savers, potentially leading to greater returns and reduced risk. The proposal is expected to undergo a period of public comment before any final decisions are made. Industry experts are closely monitoring the developments, as the changes could significantly alter the landscape of retirement savings in the United States.

data.title: Iran Shifts University Courses Online Starting April 4
data.description: Iran will transition all university and higher education courses to an online format beginning April 4, according to PANews. This change will remain in effect until further notice, as announced by Iranian government spokesperson Mohajerani.

data.title: France Detains Two More Suspects in Foiled Paris Bombing Plot
data.description: French authorities have detained two additional individuals in relation to the thwarted bombing attempt near the Bank of America office in Paris. Bloomberg posted on X, indicating that the incident is believed to be connected to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The arrests come as part of an ongoing investigation into the attempted attack, which has heightened security concerns in the region. French officials are working to determine the full extent of the plot and any potential links to international groups. The situation remains under close scrutiny as authorities continue their efforts to ensure public safety.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Stocks Open Higher Amid Aluminum Industry Tensions
data.description: U.S. stocks opened higher on March 30, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average rising by 0.78%, the S&P 500 index increasing by 0.77%, and the Nasdaq Composite climbing by 0.7%. According to Jin10, shares of Alcoa Corporation surged by 7.9% following reports of Iranian attacks on major aluminum plants in Bahrain and the UAE. The geopolitical tensions have raised concerns about the stability of aluminum supply chains, impacting market sentiment.

data.title: Alphabet Shares Expected to Rebound Above $310 by April 2026
data.description: Alphabet shares are projected to close above $310 by April 2026, despite a nearly 14% decline year to date. According to NS3.AI, the forecast is supported by a 14-day Relative Strength Index (RSI) of 18.2 and the upcoming Q1 2026 earnings release on April 23, which could drive a recovery towards the $315 resistance level.

data.title: European stock indices and precious metals rose across the board, while the US dollar came under pressure and fell.
data.description: According to the latest data from Gate, gold prices rose to $4,569.26 per ounce, a daily increase of 1.70%. Silver prices rose to $71.271 per ounce, a daily increase of 2.30%. The BVIX (BTC Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 54.06, a daily increase of 0.26%. The EVIX (ETH Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 73.61, a daily decrease of 1.06%. In the foreign exchange market, the USD/CNH exchange rate fell 0.04% to 6.91630. The USD/JPY exchange rate fell 0.55% to 159.366. In global stock indices, the Euro Stoxx 50 rose 1.56% to 5,539.15 points; the UK FTSE 100 rose 1.95% to 10,084.99 points; and the German DAX 40 rose 1.50% to 22,434.31 points. In commodities, WTI crude oil rose 0.58% to $101.96 per barrel, and Brent crude oil rose 0.83% to $111.63 per barrel. Gate allows users to directly trade traditional financial market products on its platform, providing a one-stop solution for precious metals, forex, global equity CFDs, major indices, and commodities, achieving deep integration of crypto assets and traditional financial assets. Gate TradFi features have been fully integrated into the Gate App and Web platform, allowing users to easily participate in global asset price trading without switching platforms, unlocking more strategies and opportunities outside the crypto market, and continuously enhancing their diversified asset allocation experience.

data.title: Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac Stocks Surge at Market Open
data.description: Fannie Mae's stock price surged by 14% at the market open, while Freddie Mac saw an 11% increase. According to Jin10, this significant rise in stock prices reflects investor optimism and market confidence in these government-sponsored enterprises. The increase comes amid broader market movements and economic indicators that are influencing investor behavior. Analysts are closely monitoring these developments to assess their potential impact on the housing finance sector and the overall economy.

data.title: Philippines Boosts Petroleum Stockpile Amid Global Supply Challenges
data.description: The Philippines has announced an increase in its petroleum product reserves to cover 51 days of consumption. Bloomberg posted on X that this move comes as the nation, heavily reliant on imports, seeks alternative suppliers due to ongoing conflicts in Iran. The decision reflects the country's proactive approach to securing energy resources amidst global uncertainties.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 68,000 USDT with a 2.18% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 30, 2026, 13:22 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 68,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 68,127.976563 USDT, with a narrowed 2.18% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Bill Ackman Highlights Investment Opportunities Amid Global Market Conditions
data.description: On March 30, renowned hedge fund manager Bill Ackman shared his insights on social media regarding current investment opportunities. According to BlockBeats, Ackman emphasized that some of the world's top companies are trading at exceptionally low prices. He advised investors to disregard mainstream media narratives, suggesting that the ongoing global conflict is one of the most lopsided in history, with outcomes likely to benefit both the United States and the world. Ackman expressed optimism about the potential for substantial peace dividends and described the present as one of the best times to invest in high-quality assets, urging investors to ignore bearish sentiments.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Optimistic About Strait of Hormuz Reopening
data.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent expressed optimism regarding the potential reopening of the Strait of Hormuz for cargo ship passage. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Bessent's remarks about the administration's ongoing efforts to tackle the global oil supply shortage. The reopening of this crucial maritime route is seen as a significant step towards stabilizing oil distribution worldwide.
